questions.question: What does `break` do in a `switch`?
options: Exit the switch block and prevent fall-through
questions.answer: Exit the switch block and prevent fall-through

questions.question: What happens if you omit `break` in a case?
options: Execution falls through to the next case
questions.answer: Execution falls through to the next case

questions.question: When is the `default` case executed?
questions.answer: When no other case matches

questions.question: Which type CANNOT be used in a switch expression?
questions.answer: `double`

questions.question: Is `default` required in a switch?
questions.answer: No, optional

questions.question: Can you stack multiple case labels for the same block?
options: Yes, by listing them before the code block
No, each needs its own block
questions.answer: Yes, by listing them before the code block

questions.question: What must follow the `case` keyword?
questions.answer: A constant integral expression

questions.question: If `x=2` and case 2 has no break, what runs after it?
questions.answer: case 3 code (fall-through)

questions.question: A `switch` statement is a form of:
questions.answer: Multi-way branching

questions.question: The `switch` statement tests for:
questions.answer: Equality only

questions.question: What does `[[fallthrough]]` do in C++17?
options: Marks intentional fall-through to suppress compiler warnings
questions.answer: Marks intentional fall-through to suppress compiler warnings

questions.question: Which type requires `Color::Red` syntax in switch cases?
questions.answer: `enum class` (scoped)

questions.question: Can `std::string` be used as a switch expression?
questions.answer: No, only integral types

questions.question: What does `switch (int n = f(); n)` (C++17 init) do?
options: Declares n visible only within the switch block, branches on n
questions.answer: Declares n visible only within the switch block, branches on n

questions.question: If you want exhaustive matching of an enum, what compiler feature helps?
options: Compiler warning `-Wswitch` or equivalent when cases are missing
questions.answer: Both A and B

questions.question: Can you declare variables inside a switch case?
options: Yes, but their scope must be bounded with braces `{}`
questions.answer: Yes, but their scope must be bounded with braces `{}`

questions.question: What is a jump table optimization for switch?
options: Compiler may generate an array of jump targets making switch O(1) instead of O(n) chain
questions.answer: Compiler may generate an array of jump targets making switch O(1) instead of O(n) chain

questions.question: With enum class, can you compare without switch? E.g., `color == Color::Red`?
questions.answer: Yes, using `==`

questions.question: What is 'switch fall-through' behavior?
options: Execution continues into the next case when break is absent
questions.answer: Execution continues into the next case when break is absent

questions.question: Can you use a `return` statement inside a switch case?
questions.answer: Yes — it exits the function

questions.question: Does C++ switch support pattern matching like Rust `match`?
options: No — switch only tests equality on integral types
questions.answer: No — switch only tests equality on integral types

questions.question: What does `std::visit` do with a `std::variant`?
options: Calls the appropriate lambda/function for the currently held variant alternative
questions.answer: Calls the appropriate lambda/function for the currently held variant alternative

questions.question: When is a switch compiled to a jump table vs an if-chain?
options: Dense integer ranges → jump table; sparse ranges may use if-chain or other strategy
questions.answer: Dense integer ranges → jump table; sparse ranges may use if-chain or other strategy

questions.question: What is the `overloaded` pattern used with `std::visit`?
options: A helper struct that inherits lambdas to enable multi-lambda visitation
questions.answer: A helper struct that inherits lambdas to enable multi-lambda visitation

questions.question: What is Duff's device?
options: An intentional fall-through loop unrolling trick within a switch statement
questions.answer: An intentional fall-through loop unrolling trick within a switch statement

questions.question: State pattern in OOP vs switch — what is the main difference?
options: State pattern uses virtual dispatch to eliminate switches on type/state
State pattern is only for threads
questions.answer: State pattern uses virtual dispatch to eliminate switches on type/state

questions.question: What is 'exhaustive matching' and why is it desirable?
options: Ensuring all possible cases are handled so no input falls through unhandled
Matching only the most common case
questions.answer: Ensuring all possible cases are handled so no input falls through unhandled

questions.question: Which C++ standard introduced the `[[fallthrough]]` attribute?
If C++ switch expressions must be integral/enum, how do you branch on strings efficiently?
options: Hash the string and switch on the hash
Both A and C are common approaches
questions.answer: Both A and C are common approaches

questions.question: What does `std::get<int>(variant)` do vs `std::visit`?
options: `std::get` extracts the value, throwing `std::bad_variant_access` if wrong type; `std::visit` dispatches automatically
questions.answer: `std::get` extracts the value, throwing `std::bad_variant_access` if wrong type; `std::visit` dispatches automatically
